Providers may return a cleanup: (T, func(), error) and (T, func()); the
generated code guards nil and defers release in reverse construction order.
-- go.mod --
module app

go 1.26
-- main.go --
package main

import "os"

func main() { os.Exit(run()) }
-- web/web.go --
package web

import "net/http"

type DB struct{}

//fabrik:provider
func NewDB() (*DB, func(), error) { db := &DB{}; return db, func() {}, nil }

type Cache struct{}

//fabrik:provider
func NewCache(db *DB) (*Cache, func()) { return &Cache{}, nil }

type Index struct{}

//fabrik:provider
func NewIndex(c *Cache) (*Index, error) { return &Index{}, nil }

type H struct {
	DB    *DB
	Cache *Cache
	Index *Index
}

//fabrik:http GET /x
func (h *H) Get(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {}
-- want/main.gen.go --
// Code generated by fabrik. DO NOT EDIT.
// Regenerate with: fabrik wire

package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"

	"github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/router"

	"app/web"
)

func run() int {
	if err := func() error {
		// Providers
		webDB, webDBClose, err := web.NewDB()
		if err != nil {
			return err
		}
		if webDBClose != nil {
			defer webDBClose()
		}
		webCache, webCacheClose := web.NewCache(webDB)
		if webCacheClose != nil {
			defer webCacheClose()
		}
		webIndex, err := web.NewIndex(webCache)
		if err != nil {
			return err
		}
		webH := &web.H{
			DB:    webDB,
			Cache: webCache,
			Index: webIndex,
		}

		r := router.New()

		// Register
		r.Method("GET", "/x", webH.Get)
		return nil
	}(); err != nil {
		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "app:", err)
		return 1
	}
	return 0
}
